title: Dementia Science Programme: Incidence/ Prevalence/ Risk/ Intervention analysis of dementia and basic research thereof
sites: India, 2 population sites; 4 hospitals in across 5 states (INCLEN-coordinator)
summary: The programme aims to improve outcomes of non-communicable diseases, including dementia, in India through evidence-based policies and multidisciplinary research. It established two population-based cohorts and four hospital registries, enrolling over 7,600 participants and 754 patients. The study focuses on identifying biomarkers for diagnosis and prognosis using advanced testing platforms and genetic analysis. A total of 450 samples across dementia, MCI, and control groups are being analyzed. INCLEN collaborates with NBRC for MRI scans, with 110 completed so far. Biorepository and data storage systems have been developed, with 450 biological samples collected for future research and long-term study of neurodegenerative progression.

title: To determine the causes of under-five child deaths and stillbirths in north-eastern region of India by minimally invasive tissue sampling (MITS) technique: An ICMR Task Force Study
sites: India, three sites in North-Eastern region of India
summary: India has made significant progress in reducing the child mortality and perinatal mortality over last decade, but the progress has been uneven. Additionally the nutritional deficiencies like Thiamine deficiency have been also identified as potential risk factors and cause of unexplained infant deaths. This proposal aims to better characterise the causes of under-five child deaths and stillbirths in the NER at three sites; Guwahati, Dibrugarh and Agartala. The parents/family members shall be approached for conduct of MITS. During the MITS body fluids and tissue samples from targeted organs shall be collected. The findings shall better inform to improving the clinical practices, mother and child program and policy making in the region and at national level.

title: Maternal micronutrient deficiency in India: Program and policy implications
summary: The current proposal is an effort to synthesize the available evidences on the current status of maternal micronutrient deficiencies in India, and the experiences from various interventions. This project also aims to undertake a deep-dive situational analysis on the programmatic implementation related to maternal nutrition and micronutrient supplementation. The evidence generated from this project shall inform further programmatic actions, implementation researches and targeted interventions for addressing the maternal micronutrient deficiencies in India.

title: Effect of Multiple Micronutrient Supplementation (MMS) during Pregnancy on maternal anemia, pregnancy and neonatal outcomes in India: A Community based RCT (Preparatory grant)
sites: India (In preparation for six-site RCT for India-specific MMS in pregnant women)
summary: This proposal focuses on accomplishing the preparatory activities for smooth conduct and execution of a large and complex multisite trial in India to determine the maternal and neonatal impacts of MMS supplementation during pregnancy. The planning phase of the grant shall focus on: preparation of the detailed protocol and data collection and management system; establishing the clinical trial sites network; and use of a MMS product composition tailored to Indian challenges. All these shall make the sites ready for undertaking regulatory trial.

title: Phase III, Multicentre, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate Efficacy of Probiotic Supplementation for Prevention of Neonatal Sepsis in 0-2 Months old Low Birth Weight Infants in India (The 'ProSPoNS' Study)
sites: India, six sites (Pune, Meerut, New Delhi, Bhubaneshwar, Puducherry, Wardha) and two labs at New Delhi
summary: Prevention of sepsis among the high-risk, vulnerable low birth weight neonates by a newer intervention with probiotic supplementation is promising. A multi-center study with 6144 infants randomized to receive Vivomixx probiotic or a placebo. Findings: The incidence of PSBI was significantly lower in the probiotic than the placebo group (4.66% vs. 6.17%), representing a 25% relative risk reduction. Daily probiotic supplementation significantly reduced the incidence of PSBI in LBW infants, supporting its role as a safe and effective infection prevention strategy in community settings within LMICs.

title: Assessing the Pandemic/Epidemic Readiness of Primary Health Care System in India
summary: This partnership exercise is to develop protocols and tools for assessing the Pandemic/Epidemic Readiness of the Primary Healthcare System in India. Phase I focuses on reviewing global best practices and field testing tools in pilot districts. Phase II aims to rollout these protocols nationwide to identify key enablers and challenges for sustainability within India's Primary Health Care system.

title: India Child Growth Standards Research Initiative: A Prospective Cohort Study to Develop Growth and Development Standards in 0 to 24 months Indian Children (UNNATI: Upgrading Norms for Growth and Development Assessment of Indian Children)
sites: India, 6 sites (INCLEN is responsible for East Khasi Hills site, Meghalaya)
summary: This prospective cohort study with children from six regions of India aims to measure childhood growth and development from 0-24 months in an environment without major biological or economic constraints. The study seeks to develop normative neurodevelopmental charts for Indian children and growth curves for term Small for Gestational Age (SGA) babies, following participants fortnightly for the first 2 months and then monthly/bimonthly till 24 months.

title: Expanding antimicrobial resistance research and surveillance activities in the secondary level hospitals (ICMR NHRP)
sites: India – two districts and one medical college in Odisha
summary: The emergence of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) poses a significant public health crisis. This initiative expands AMR surveillance to secondary care settings in Odisha (Khordha and Puri), supported by KIMS-Bhubaneswar. The project establishes mechanisms to connect secondary facilities with tertiary settings for quality susceptibility data collection and antibiotic stewardship, aiming to address limited resources and expertise in documenting local antibiograms.

title: National Certification Committee for Polio Eradication (NCCPE) and National Verification Committee for Measles-Rubella
funder: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (Govt of India)
summary: This independent committee monitors polio eradication progress in India as part of the Global Polio Eradication Initiative (GPEI). The committee assesses Polio incidence data and recommends system improvements. It also handles monitoring measles eradication and congenital rubella control in the country, preparing annual and country-level certification reports for WHO.

title: Catalyze early detection and management of cancers through the National Program for Prevention & Control of Cancer, Diabetes, Cardiovascular Diseases and Stroke (NPCDCS) in East Khasi Hills District
summary: This implementation research aims to strengthen cancer screening (oral, breast, and cervical) in Meghalaya, where coverage is currently around 1%. Applying human-centered principles and nexus planning, the project co-creates strategies to improve program uptake. It involves capacity building for frontline workers, community engagement for tobacco cessation, and overseeing implementation within the existing health system framework.

title: Quality of intrapartum and immediate postpartum care of mothers and newborns: an implementation research to catalyze performance of LaQshya program in six districts of India
summary: LaQshya aims to fast-track the quality of maternal and newborn care in Indian health facilities. This implementation research identifies barriers in quality certification and co-develops strategies for labor rooms and maternal OTs. It includes handholding and capacity building across six districts to improve respectful maternity care and neonatal services as per national guidelines.

title: Sankalp: Strengthening Program Implementation and Monitoring to Achieve Single-digit Neonatal Mortality (ICMR NHRP)
funder: Gates Foundation and Indian Council of Medical Research
summary: Known as 'Mission Single-Digit', this project targets high-NMR districts like Purnea, Bihar. It uses implementation research to develop decentralized planning models and optimize evidence-based practices for mothers and newborns. The goal is to accelerate the reduction of the Neonatal Mortality Rate to single digits through improved coverage of key health interventions.

title: Implementation Research to improve maternal health care interventions around delivery on identification, risk stratification and the occurrence of stillbirth across three distinct demographic & geographic settings in India.
summary: This research addresses India's high stillbirth burden through sequential mixed-methods research including NEXUS planning and minimally invasive tissue sampling (MITS). It identifies system barriers and co-implements high-risk pregnancy identification strategies across diverse settings to establish culturally specific frameworks for stillbirth reduction and surveillance.

title: Convergence and integration of programmes and leveraging technology for enabling healthcare teams for delivery of equitable, high-quality primary healthcare services through Health and Wellness Centers (HWCs) (East Khasi Hills)
summary: Focusing on Meghalaya, this study uses the CFIR and RE-AIM frameworks to bridge the 'know-do' gap in Comprehensive Primary Healthcare (CPHC). It co-develops technology-enabled implementation models focusing on inter-departmental convergence to achieve high population-level coverage, quality, and equity through HWCs.

title: Convergence and integration of programmes and leveraging technology for enabling healthcare teams for delivery of equitable, high-quality primary healthcare services through Health and Wellness Centers (HWCs) (Jagatsinghpur)
summary: Parallel to the Meghalaya study, this research in Jagatsinghpur, Odisha, aims to optimize CPHC delivery. It evaluates the impact of convergence-focused models and technology integration on service quality and equity, providing evidence for context-specific implementation across different Indian states.

title: Leveraging genome sequencing to prevent hospital-acquired infections with Carbapenem-Resistant Acinetobacter baumannii (CRAb)
summary: This study uses genomic surveillance to understand the transmission dynamics of CRAb across six tertiary hospitals in Odisha. By mapping phylogenetic lineages, the project designs and implements contextualized infection control strategies, assessing the feasibility of integrated surveillance to tackle healthcare-associated outbreaks.

title: Implementation Research to strengthen Comprehensive Newborn Screening component of RBSK and estimate the burden of Congenital Heart Disease (CHD)
funder: Submitted to Indian Council of Medical Research
summary: Targeting congenital structural heart defects, this proposal aims to strengthen the RBSK program's screening efficiency. It estimates CHD prevalence across diverse Indian landscapes and establishes referral pathways, integrated with whole exome sequencing to study genomic and epigenetic markers associated with these diseases.

title: Strategies for Enhanced Population Engagement and Health Care Seeking for Prevention and Control of Hypertension and Diabetes in India: The STEPS-INDIA Multi-Centre Study
summary: This multi-center cluster randomized trial evaluates the effectiveness of enhanced community engagement strategies (eCES) for controlling hypertension and diabetes. It involves systematic reviews, formative research, and piloting models to improve screening adherence, lifestyle changes, and long-term glycemic control among adults in primary healthcare settings.

title: Development and evaluation of effectiveness of improved take home foods in combination with behaviour change intervention to improve nutritional status in children (NECCTAR Trial)
summary: The NECCTAR trial evaluates an integrated package of improved take-home foods (THR) and social-behavior change communication (SBCC) for children aged 6-36 months. This cluster randomized study aims to reduce undernutrition, wasting, and stunting while documenting impact on caregiver knowledge and community health practices.

title: Dynamics of Late Foetal and Neonatal Mortality in the Indian Context
funder: NIH, USA (Collaboration with University of Pennsylvania)
summary: This study collects prospective data on stillbirths and neonatal deaths by gestational age in Bareilly. It examines excess mortality patterns vs. data errors and investigates risks associated with small-for-gestational-age and preterm births, leveraging real-time pregnancy tracking by community health workers.

title: International Network for Vaccine Safety Surveillance (INNOVATE): An Integrated Population and Sentinel Hospital-Based Vaccine Safety Surveillance
sites: 6 countries (India, Indonesia, South Africa, Ethiopia, Kenya, Brazil)
summary: INNOVATE aims to strengthen active AEFI surveillance in LMICs. By establishing population cohorts of ~50,000 adults per site, the project documents serious adverse events and hospitalizations following immunization, creating a robust platform for long-term monitoring of both new and existing vaccines.

title: Consortium to explore pre-implementation questions on MR-MAPs (Measles-Rubella microarray patches)
summary: This research consortium explores the pre-implementation landscape for microarray patches for Measles-Rubella vaccination. It focuses on research framework development, primary data collection, and consolidated analysis to understand how this innovative delivery platform can be scaled through existing immunization programs.

title: SORT-IT to Empower NITAGs for Evidence Based Decision Making in South East Asia Region
summary: This initiative aims to strengthen the technical capacity of National Immunization Technical Advisory Groups (NITAGs) for data analysis. It provides a framework for using programmatic data in operational research to enhance immunization outcomes and rational decision-making across member states in South East Asia.

title: Community based Surveillance to estimate incidence and sero prevalence of acute febrile illness with focus on Dengue and Chikungunya
summary: Completed: This prospective cohort study across 10 sites estimated etiological diagnosis of fever. It focused on malaria, dengue, and chikungunya, documenting high seroprevalence at baseline (97% in Palwal). The network became part of DBT's DRIVEN network, conducting follow-ups to evaluate vaccine safety and disease burden.

title: SOMAARTH-e-Data Management Platform for quality assured electronic data collection and archival of surveillance sites
summary: Ongoing: INCLEN developed an enhanced e-platform to manage the full life-cycle of research studies. The platform integrated heterogeneous data from six sites to study epidemiology and community transmission dynamics, supporting COVID-19 burden assessments and GIS-based demographic surveillance.

title: Indian network of population based COVID 19 vaccine safety surveillance (INVACS)
funder: Global Vaccine Data Network (primary funder DCD)
summary: Completed: INVACS documented background rates of AESIs and serious adverse events following COVID-19 vaccination. By following family-based cohorts of 500,000+ persons, the project captured specific AESIs like TTS and myocarditis through systematic hospital screening to provide an evidence base for vaccine safety.

title: In-depth review of interventions for the prevention of anemia in India and select states to inform future programming
sites: India, 15 states for evaluation and 2 for detailed collection
summary: Completed: This project reviewed the Anemia Mukt Bharat initiative, identifying programmatic failures and proposing refinements. Recommendations emphasized 'T3' (Test, Treat, Track) strategies and digital invasive hemoglobinometers, informing NITI Aayog on improving inter-departmental coordination and behavior change campaigns.

title: Applying minimally invasive tissue sampling (MITS) to document the causes of death across different age groups and stillbirths
summary: Stopped: This project applied MITS for identification of Causes of Death in children and adults. While it successfully conducted 27 MITS cases, applying molecular diagnosis and pathology, the study was prematurely terminated due to FCRA challenges despite promising results in characterizing under-identified death causes.

title: Understanding current childhood pneumonia management practices: An Observational Study in SOMAARTH Palwal District
summary: Completed: This study tracked children with pneumonia attending primary and community health centers to document care practices and outcomes. It identified variances in clinical care and influencers of care-seeking, providing a database of community and facility-level adherence to WHO pneumonia management guidelines.

title: Neurodevelopmental Disorders Screening Tool (NDST) for 1-10 year Old Children: Machine Learning based Algorithms
summary: Completed: INCLEN and IBM developed 'AI-Neuro-DOST', an algorithm for early NDD detection. Using machine learning on Indian data, the tool achieved >85% sensitivity and specificity for five common disorders, demonstrating potential for early diagnosis in resource-limited settings through simple questionnaire-based screening.

title: Nutrient and microbiota characterization of traditional fermented rice-water (Torani) and its impact on maternal nutrition
summary: Completed: This study characterized the microbiota and nutrient content of 'Torani' (fermented water rice) in Odisha. Findings showed positive changes in gut microbiome and serum iron status among reproductive-age women, suggesting traditional fermented food as a viable option for maternal nutritional modulation.

title: Harmonized One-health Trans-Species Transmission of Antibiotics Resistance: Community Level Transmission of CREC
summary: Completed: This study investigated carbapenem-resistant E. coli transmission across humans, animals, and environment in Odisha. It found 20.5% carriage among healthy humans, with contact with animals and socio-economic status as key drivers. This is the first evidence of intra-host transmission at the household level in India under One Health.

title: Diagnosis of pneumonia and respiratory illnesses using digital stethoscope and algorithm in children and adults
summary: Ongoing: This project uses digital stethoscopes linked to mobile apps to generate a database of breath sounds. AI/ML algorithms analyze audio parameters (pitch, frequency) to help doctors differentiate pneumonia from other conditions, addressing inter-listener variability and subjectivity in traditional auscultation.

title: To generate a list of priority research areas in the field of vaccine and immunization for the South-East Asia region
summary: Completed: Using the CHNRI method, INCLEN identified 25 research priorities for SEAR immunization. Key areas include vaccine hesitancy, digital data management, and logistics. The project provides a strategic roadmap for member states to align with the Immunization Agenda 2030 and address regional challenges.

title: Geo-Health Software Application: Spatial Data Management System for health surveys
summary: Completed: INCGeo-health is an open-source spatial management system supporting health demographics. It features web dashboards and an Android app for field data collection and shapefile editing, reducing reliance on paper-based collection and enabling real-time monitoring of field staff positions.

title: Developing a Public Health Informatics Platform in India for a systems View of Health and Diseases
summary: Completed: This project created open platforms for public health epidemiology, collating diverse data sources from rural and urban India. It emphasized childhood and adolescent health, bringing together data from the Pune Birth Cohort and various Symbiosis University health checkups to provide a systems understanding of population health.

title: Safety, Tolerability and Immunogenicity of GEMCOVAC-OM as a booster in Subjects 18 Years of Age and Older
summary: Completed: This Phase II/III study evaluated the booster efficacy of the mRNA vaccine GEMCOVAC-OM (Omicron variant sequence) in people previously vaccinated with Covaxin or Covishield. The study enrolled 3140 subjects, demonstrating favorable safety and immunogenicity profiles for the mRNA-based booster dose.

title: Post Marketing Surveillance Study to assess the safety of SIIPL's qHPV vaccine (CERVAVAC)
sites: India, 2 sites for INCLEN (Shillong and Palwal)
summary: Completed: This active monitoring study assessed the safety of CERVAVAC in children (9-14) and adults (15-26). In Palwal, 75 participants completed 3 doses with no serious vaccine-related adverse events. The study demonstrated good vaccine feasibility and adherence in rural and tribal settings in India.

title: Clinical Study to Evaluate Immunogenicity and Safety of CERVAVAC vs Gardasil in Girls and Women
summary: Completed: This observer-blind clinical trial compared the immunogenicity of CERVAVAC with the established Gardasil vaccine. Conducted in Shillong, the study followed 2000 subjects across different age cohorts, providing critical safety and comparative data for the Indian-made qHPV vaccine.

title: Safety and immunogenicity of Inactivated Japanese Encephalitis Vaccine (JEEV) in adults and infants
summary: Completed: This Phase III study evaluated the safety of JEEV in infants (9-12 months) and older adults (49-70). It tested co-administration with the MMR vaccine, providing strong safety and immunogenicity results for late-infancy vaccination schedules and elderly population immunity against Japanese Encephalitis.
